title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Tournament Format Explained
summary: The 2026 World Cup will feature 48 teams for the first time, with 12 groups of 4 teams each. The top two from each group plus the eight best third-placed teams advance to the Round of 32.
author: FIFA Communications
content: The expanded format brings more nations than ever before to the world's biggest sporting event.

title: Host Cities Ready: 16 Venues Across Three Nations
summary: From Mexico City's Estadio Azteca to New York's MetLife Stadium, all 16 venues have completed preparations for the tournament.
author: World Cup Insider
content: The 2026 World Cup spans three host nations for the first time in history.

title: Qualification Complete: All 48 Teams Confirmed
summary: The final qualifying matches have concluded, with all 48 teams now confirmed for the tournament. Several debut nations will make their first appearance.
author: Global Football Report
content: The qualification process spanned over two years across all FIFA confederations.

title: Top Players to Watch at the 2026 World Cup
summary: From rising stars to seasoned veterans, here are the players expected to make the biggest impact at this summer's tournament.
author: Sports Analytics Desk
content: The tournament promises to showcase both established superstars and breakout talents.

title: Opening Ceremony Details Announced
summary: FIFA has revealed plans for the opening ceremony at Estadio Azteca in Mexico City, featuring performances celebrating the cultures of all three host nations.
author: FIFA Communications
content: The ceremony will blend Mexican, American, and Canadian cultural traditions.

title: Sustainability Initiatives at the 2026 World Cup
summary: FIFA outlines its commitment to carbon-neutral operations, including renewable energy at venues, waste reduction programs, and sustainable transportation options.
author: Green Sports Alliance
content: The 2026 tournament aims to set new standards for sustainability in mega sporting events.
